hypothesis: Predicting DFT band gaps of MOFs using ∆-learning XGBoost models that learn the difference between GFN1-xTB and DFT values from ST-135, RACs, and APRDF descriptors will yield significantly lower prediction errors than direct XGBoost models trained to predict DFT band gaps and will match or exceed the performance of MOFTransformer and CGCNN benchmarks because the semi-empirical GFN1-xTB provides a physics-informed baseline that simplifies the residual learning task.
